steps.title: Factor out the common term
steps.content:
Each term has a factor of $2x$.
$$
2x^3+38x^2+168x=2x(x^2+19x+84)
$$

steps.title: Factor the quadratic
steps.content:
Find two positive numbers with product $84$ and sum $19$.
$$
x^2+19x+84=(x+7)(x+12)
$$
So the original expression factors as
$$
2x(x+7)(x+12)
$$

steps.title: Choose the smallest constant
steps.content: The factors of the form $x+b$ are $x+7$ and $x+12$, so the possible positive values of $b$ are $7$ and $12$. The answer is 7.
